data.content_ref: inline:{"claim":"Mass migrations of displaced coastal populations coalesced into the Sea Peoples, whose coordinated naval raids destabilized the political order of the eastern Mediterranean.","mechanism":"Geopolitical‑military destabilization through destruction of trade routes and forcing defensive overextension.","scope":{"covers":["Mycenaean Greece","Hittite Anatolia","Ugarit","Levantine city‑states"],"excludes":["Egypt"]},"forbidden":[{"case":"Contemporary Egyptian inscriptions lacking any mention of Sea Peoples before 1175 BCE.","eval":"rubric:std-hist"}],"prose_notes":"Analytic report comparing documented raids at Mycenaean citadels, Hittite annals, and Ugaritic tablets."}
